primary_sources.title: Unicode Technical Standard #39: Unicode Security Mechanisms

finding: Identifier-profile eligibility and confusable detection are independent security mechanisms. A clean confusable result must not override a restricted or incompletely evaluated identifier, and profile eligibility must not override a skeleton collision.
uncertainty: The artifact composes upstream decisions but does not itself implement the General Security Profile, skeleton generation, script resolution, or full UTS #39 conformance.
design_implication: Admission must be conjunctive and fail closed: complete profile evidence, profile eligibility, complete confusables evidence, and skeleton uniqueness are all mandatory.

verification.method: isolated Node test run using an exact functional copy of the committed module and tests

falsification_route: Demonstrate any input for which the policy allows despite an incomplete upstream mechanism, profile rejection, or skeleton collision; or rejects when all four mandatory booleans are true.
privacy_boundary: Contains only public standards metadata, abstract security policy, repository paths, and commit identifiers.
